agent_knobs.name: bilinear_form
agent_knobs.description: a(u,v) = epsilon*inner(grad(u),grad(v))*dx + dot(beta,grad(u))*v*dx; f derived from MMS

agent_knobs.name: convection_direction
agent_knobs.description: beta=[1,0,0] is purely x-direction advection in 3D

agent_knobs.name: diagonal_flow
agent_knobs.description: All three velocity components equal; full 3D convection coupling in the bilinear form

agent_knobs.name: peclet_number
agent_knobs.description: Pe = |beta|*h/epsilon >> 1; spurious oscillations without upwind/SUPG stabilization

agent_knobs.name: supg_requirement
agent_knobs.description: SUPG adds tau*(beta.grad(v))*(beta.grad(u) - epsilon*div(grad(u)))*dx to bilinear form

agent_knobs.name: high_pe
agent_knobs.description: Very high Peclet number; Galerkin discretization is unstable without stabilization

agent_knobs.name: zero_convection
agent_knobs.description: beta=[0,0,0]; problem is symmetric SPD, CG solver applicable

agent_knobs.name: diffusion_only
agent_knobs.description: f = 3*pi^2*sin(pi*x)*sin(pi*y)*sin(pi*z); same as Poisson with unit source scaling

agent_knobs.name: unequal_components
agent_knobs.description: beta_x=2 dominates; different Pe in each spatial direction tests correct 3D dot(beta,grad(u)) assembly

agent_knobs.name: supg_tau
agent_knobs.description: SUPG parameter tau = h/(2*|beta|); AI must use 3D |beta| = sqrt(2^2+0.5^2+0.3^2) ≈ 2.09

agent_knobs.name: time_discretization
agent_knobs.description: Backward Euler: (u_n - u_{n-1})/dt + beta.grad(u_n) - epsilon*Laplacian(u_n) = f(t_n)

agent_knobs.name: full_3d_transport
agent_knobs.description: Diagonal 3D transport; all three gradient components contribute to convection in the weak form

agent_knobs.name: transient_supg
agent_knobs.description: Transient SUPG bilinear form: (u*v + dt*epsilon*inner(grad,grad) + dt*dot(beta,grad(u))*v + dt*tau*dot(beta,grad(v))*(dot(beta,grad(u))-epsilon*div(grad(u))))*dx

agent_knobs.name: time_source
agent_knobs.description: Source f(t) evaluated at current time t_n; time-dependent RHS in backward Euler

agent_knobs.name: polynomial_solution
agent_knobs.description: Bubble function u=x(1-x)y(1-y)z(1-z) is zero on all boundaries; P2 elements can represent this exactly

agent_knobs.name: source_derivation
agent_knobs.description: f = -epsilon*(Laplacian of bubble) + beta.grad(bubble); oracle derives this symbolically via sympy

agent_knobs.name: time_integration
agent_knobs.description: Backward Euler with dt=0.005 for 20 steps; source f=(3*pi^2-1)*u must be included

agent_knobs.name: kappa_value
agent_knobs.description: kappa=2 appears in the bilinear form a(u,v)=integral(kappa*grad(u)*grad(v)dx) + mass term

agent_knobs.name: variable_kappa
agent_knobs.description: kappa=1+x+y+z requires div(kappa*grad(u)) in weak form, not just kappa*Laplacian(u)

agent_knobs.name: source_term
agent_knobs.description: f = u_t - div((1+x+y+z)*grad(u)); must account for gradient of kappa

agent_knobs.name: initial_condition
agent_knobs.description: Initial condition is superposition of two Fourier modes with different decay rates

agent_knobs.name: time_stepping
agent_knobs.description: Smaller dt=0.0025 for accuracy with faster-decaying high-frequency mode

agent_knobs.name: num_steps
agent_knobs.description: 100 time steps from t=0 to t=1; test long-time stability of implicit scheme

agent_knobs.name: long_time_solution
agent_knobs.description: At t=1: u=sin(pi*x)*sin(pi*y)*sin(pi*z)*exp(-1)~0.368*initial; source f=(3*pi^2-1)*u must be included

agent_knobs.name: initial_condition
agent_knobs.description: Sharp Gaussian at (0.5,0.5,0.5) diffuses outward; set zero Dirichlet BC

agent_knobs.name: time_stepping
agent_knobs.description: Fine dt=0.0025 needed to capture early rapid spreading of Gaussian

agent_knobs.name: kappa_value
agent_knobs.description: kappa=10 multiplies the Laplacian term; bilinear form stiffness matrix needs correct kappa scaling

agent_knobs.name: source_term
agent_knobs.description: Large source f=(10*3*pi^2-1)*u=(295.2)*u; AI must correctly derive this from the MMS

agent_knobs.name: kappa_expression
agent_knobs.description: kappa oscillates between 0.1 and 1.9; div(kappa*grad(u)) includes cross-derivative term from dkappa/dx

agent_knobs.name: source_term_derivation
agent_knobs.description: f = u_t - div(kappa*grad(u)); kappa depends only on x so dkappa/dy=dkappa/dz=0

agent_knobs.name: source_term
agent_knobs.description: Constant volumetric heat source f=1 throughout cube with zero-temperature walls

agent_knobs.name: steady_state
agent_knobs.description: At t=0.1 solution approaches quasi-steady state (solving -Laplacian(u)=1)

agent_knobs.name: contrast_ratio
agent_knobs.description: kappa ranges from 1 to 50; regions near corner (1,1,1) diffuse 50x faster than near origin

agent_knobs.name: preconditioner
agent_knobs.description: High-contrast coefficients in parabolic problems benefit from AMG for the spatial solve

agent_knobs.name: wavenumber
agent_knobs.description: k=1 is well below pollution threshold; standard CG with moderate mesh suffices

agent_knobs.name: wavenumber
agent_knobs.description: k=3; rule of thumb: at least 10 elements per wavelength (lambda=2*pi/k)

agent_knobs.name: solver_type
agent_knobs.description: Helmholtz is indefinite; use GMRES not CG

agent_knobs.name: wavenumber
agent_knobs.description: k=5 starts to exhibit numerical pollution; need h*k^2 to be small

agent_knobs.name: preconditioner
agent_knobs.description: ILU may not converge for indefinite systems; consider direct solver or shifted Laplacian

agent_knobs.name: bc_handling
agent_knobs.description: Non-zero Dirichlet BC from Gaussian profile on all 6 cube faces

agent_knobs.name: source_derivation
agent_knobs.description: f = -Laplacian(u) - k^2*u must be derived from the Gaussian MMS

agent_knobs.name: wavenumber
agent_knobs.description: k=2 with polynomial solution; verify sign of -k^2*u term

agent_knobs.name: zero_bc
agent_knobs.description: Polynomial u=x(1-x)y(1-y)z(1-z) is naturally zero on all faces

agent_knobs.name: wavenumber
agent_knobs.description: Ensure k=3 appears in the -k^2*u term of the weak form

agent_knobs.name: solver_stability
agent_knobs.description: k=4: Helmholtz becomes increasingly indefinite; GMRES+ILU may need restart parameter tuning

agent_knobs.name: mesh_resolution
agent_knobs.description: At k=4 with P2 elements, need roughly h < 0.1 to control pollution

agent_knobs.name: bc_nonzero
agent_knobs.description: Cosine BC is non-zero on all faces; must correctly interpolate Dirichlet values

agent_knobs.name: linear_solution
agent_knobs.description: Linear u=x+y+z has zero Laplacian; f reduces to -k^2*(x+y+z); P1 elements give exact solution

agent_knobs.name: degree_1_sufficient
agent_knobs.description: P1 elements exactly represent linear solutions

agent_knobs.name: mesh_resolution
agent_knobs.description: k=6 with P2: pollution rule h*k^{3/2} < C requires very fine mesh; at least h=1/28

agent_knobs.name: direct_solver
agent_knobs.description: Iterative solvers often struggle at k=6; consider direct LU (mumps) for reliability

agent_knobs.name: vector_fem_space
agent_knobs.description: VectorFunctionSpace of degree 2; 3 dofs per node in 3D

agent_knobs.name: full_3d_coupling
agent_knobs.description: All components nonzero; off-diagonal Lame stress terms couple all three displacement fields

agent_knobs.name: lame_params
agent_knobs.description: E=1, nu=0.3 => mu=0.385, lam=0.577; must derive from E,nu in weak form

agent_knobs.name: near_incompressible
agent_knobs.description: nu=0.45 gives lam/mu = 2*nu/(1-2*nu) = 9; volumetric locking risk with low-order elements

agent_knobs.name: stiffness_scale
agent_knobs.description: E=1000 scales all stresses by 1000x; lam=576.9, mu=384.6; large magnitude in bilinear form

agent_knobs.name: conditioned_solver
agent_knobs.description: High stiffness leads to ill-conditioned system; AMG preconditioner critical

agent_knobs.name: polynomial_mms
agent_knobs.description: Polynomial fields with u=0 on boundary; P2 can represent these exactly — tests correct weak form derivation

agent_knobs.name: shear_coupling
agent_knobs.description: Different polynomial shapes in each component tests correct off-diagonal sigma coupling

agent_knobs.name: soft_material
agent_knobs.description: E=0.1: mu=0.0385, lam=0.0577; small stresses for same displacement field; lam/mu ratio identical to E=1 case

agent_knobs.name: scale_invariance
agent_knobs.description: Solution shape is identical to E=1 case; relative error should match exactly if code uses correct lam/mu

agent_knobs.name: mixed_frequencies
agent_knobs.description: Each displacement component has a different dominant frequency direction; tests full 3D strain tensor coupling

agent_knobs.name: higher_wavenumber
agent_knobs.description: Factor-2 frequency in one direction doubles the Laplacian contribution from that direction

agent_knobs.name: shear_only
agent_knobs.description: u1=u2=0, u3!=0; non-zero eps13, eps23 but eps11=eps22=eps33=0 (no dilatation); tests shear stress assembly

agent_knobs.name: low_poisson
agent_knobs.description: nu=0.1 gives small lam/mu; bulk and shear moduli comparable in magnitude

agent_knobs.name: direct_lame

agent_knobs.name: nu_scaling
agent_knobs.description: nu=0.01: saddle-point A-block condition number is O(h^{-2}/nu)=O(100/h^2); robust solver essential

agent_knobs.name: viscous_dominated
agent_knobs.description: nu=10: viscous term 10x stronger; body force also 10x larger; solution shape same as nu=1 case

agent_knobs.name: solver_consistency
agent_knobs.description: AI code should produce identical relative error as nu=1 case if nu is applied consistently

agent_knobs.name: gravity_loading

agent_knobs.name: zero_dirichlet
agent_knobs.description: No-slip BC on all walls; u=0 on boundary; pure no-slip Stokes in a box

agent_knobs.name: multi_frequency

agent_knobs.name: pressure_gradient
agent_knobs.description: p=cos(pi*x)*cos(pi*y)*cos(pi*z); grad_p = [-pi*sin(pi*x)*cos, -pi*cos*sin, -pi*cos*cos*sin]; must include in source f

agent_knobs.name: symmetry_test
agent_knobs.description: u_mag = sqrt(u1^2+u2^2+u3^2); symmetric structure tests correct assembly of all gradient components

agent_knobs.name: nu_half
agent_knobs.description: nu=0.5 halves viscous contribution; f computed as -0.5*Laplacian(u) + grad(p)

agent_knobs.name: mixed_problem_size
agent_knobs.description: 3D Taylor-Hood P2/P1: N^3 * (3*(p+1)^3 + (p)^3) DOFs; mesh res=8 gives ~20k velocity DOFs

agent_knobs.name: anisotropic_flow
agent_knobs.description: u1 is 4x the magnitude of u2, u3; tests that AI assembles all vector components correctly

agent_knobs.name: application_driven
agent_knobs.description: Constant body force drives recirculating flow; application-relevant unlike pure MMS cases

agent_knobs.name: no_exact_solution
agent_knobs.description: Reference from fine mesh; AI must assemble correct bilinear/linear forms to match oracle reference
